stem: A vehicle travels at 48 miles per hour on a highway. What is the vehicle's speed in feet per second? (1 mile = 5,280 feet, 1 hour = 3,600 seconds)
explanation:
To convert miles per hour to feet per second, apply two conversion factors simultaneously:

$$48 \frac{\text{mi}}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{5{,}280 \text{ ft}}{1 \text{ mi}} \times \frac{1 \text{ hr}}{3{,}600 \text{ s}}$$

$$= \frac{48 \times 5{,}280}{3{,}600} = \frac{253{,}440}{3{,}600} = 70.4 \text{ ft/s}$$

The vehicle travels at **70.4 feet per second**.

distractorRationale.A: Uses a wrong miles-to-feet factor with a missing zero: student uses 528 ft per mile instead of 5,280. $48 \times 528 \div 3{,}600 = 25{,}344 \div 3{,}600 \approx 7.04$ ft/s.
distractorRationale.B: Inverts the conversion fractions: student multiplies by 3,600 and divides by 5,280 instead of the reverse. $48 \times 3{,}600 \div 5{,}280 = 172{,}800 \div 5{,}280 \approx 32.7$ ft/s.
distractorRationale.C: Uses wrong feet-per-mile factor: student substitutes 1 mile = 5,000 feet instead of the given 5,280 feet. $48 \times 5{,}000 \div 3{,}600 = 240{,}000 \div 3{,}600 \approx 66.7$ ft/s.
